rules.message: Do not use os.tmpdir() for agent-related files. Secure MCP clients (like Gemini CLI) require files to be within the project root; use a local directory like '.totem/temp/' instead.
Don't string-match the command for '-o json' to determine output format; instead, use try-parse on stdout to handle Gemini CLI responses safely.
Use fs.openSync() to get a synchronous file descriptor for spawn(). WriteStream.fd is null until the 'open' event fires, which will cause spawn() to fail.

Distinguish between ENOENT (missing binary) and command execution failure. Swallowing all errors with a generic fallback (e.g., .catch(() => ...)) can hide environment misconfigurations and missing dependencies.
Use nullish coalescing (??) instead of logical OR (||) for numeric metrics like latency or token counts, as '||' incorrectly overrides valid '0' values.
Throw an explicit error if a required environmental configuration (like a default branch) cannot be detected, rather than returning a hardcoded fallback.
Extract the shared exec → JSON.parse → schema.validate pattern into a private helper method to avoid duplicating boilerplate.
Do not duplicate [Totem Error] re-throw guards at call sites. Centralize this logic inside the shared error handler.

Do not strip ANSI escapes or control characters from MCP tool output. LLMs benefit from the formatting (like code syntax highlighting); terminal sanitization is a UI/CLI concern, not an MCP data concern.
LanceDB uses backticks (`) for case-sensitive column identifiers, not double quotes ("). Double-quoted identifiers silently produce zero matches in DataFusion filter strings.
Do not invoke 'learn' from within 'triage'. Keep commands decoupled and use 'totem run <workflow>' for composition.
Strictly enforce 'Fail Fast' over graceful degradation. Partial context assembly (e.g., using allSettled on multiple IDs) is dangerous because the LLM may hallucinate based on incomplete information.
Use @clack/prompts instead of inquirer for CLI interactions to ensure a modern visual style
Route decorative UI output (spinners, banners, tags) to stderr (e.g., console.error() or ora with stderr stream) to keep stdout reserved strictly for pipeable data.
Use dynamic imports for heavy dependencies (e.g., 'ora') within the functions that need them to avoid CLI startup performance tax.
Avoid using '${err}' in template literals as it relies on a generic toString() call. Use 'err.message' or the full error object instead to ensure consistent and informative logging.
Avoid embedding complex shell logic or pipelines in JSON configuration. Extract hook logic into a dedicated, version-controlled script (e.g., in .gemini/hooks/) and invoke that instead.
Route host hook output (briefing/shield results) to stderr instead of stdout to prevent polluting AI tool return values.

Avoid complex single-regex patterns for git command interception. Use a dual-grep approach (e.g., grep 'git' && grep -E 'push|commit') for better reliability across platforms and to avoid POSIX compatibility issues.
MCP tool returns must be wrapped in unique XML tags and escaped internal markers to prevent indirect prompt injection.
AI-provided environment variables (like $TOOL_INPUT) must be quoted in shell commands to prevent command injection.
Use a case-insensitive regex that accounts for optional internal whitespace (e.g., /<\/\s*tag\s*>/gi) when escaping XML tags to prevent prompt injection.
Use the .cjs extension for utility scripts and host integration hooks in ESM-first projects to ensure compatibility with external tools (like certain ESM loaders or host hooks).
Escape closing XML tags in prompts as <\/tag> instead of </tag> or HTML entities to prevent prompt injection and minimize parsing noise.
Do not apply terminal sanitization (like stripAnsi) to LLM prompts. Terminal stripping degrades code fidelity; use XML escaping (formatXmlResponse) for prompt safety instead.
Do not sanitize semi-trusted metadata like branch names or file paths; keep them raw to maintain prompt readability.

Avoid generic line-matching patterns (like line.startsWith('(')) when scrubbing sections from shared files. Use precise matches or unique block markers to prevent accidental removal of user-added logic.
Always quote shell variables (e.g., "$VAR" or "${VAR}") to prevent word-splitting and argument injection

Sanitize text derived from untrusted sources (like LLM outputs) before displaying in interactive CLI components to prevent ANSI escape sequence injection. Use a utility like strip-ansi if the source is not trusted.

Environment variable checks for a 'configured' state must validate non-whitespace characters (e.g., using /\S/.test(process.env.VAR)) to prevent false positives from variables exported as empty strings.
Use exact count assertions (e.g., .toBe(10)) instead of weak bounds (e.g., .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5)) for fixed collections to prevent regression errors when items are deleted.
Use exact count assertions (e.g., .toHaveLength(n)) instead of 'greater than' checks when verifying fixed sets of assets or features to catch accidental deletions.
Use temporary files instead of CLI arguments or environment variables when passing large data (like prompts) to sub-processes on Windows to avoid 'argument list too long' errors.
Use named error objects (e.g., err.name = 'NoDocsConfiguredError') instead of string matching err.message for handling skip conditions.

rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize user-provided text before persisting to files
rules.message: Sanitize ANSI escape sequences (e.g., using stripAnsi()) before persisting text to Markdown or log files to prevent terminal injection vulnerabilities.

rules.lessonHeading: When re-throwing errors in a CLI orchestrator, always
rules.message: When re-throwing errors in the CLI orchestrator, always include the '[Totem Error]' prefix in the message for consistent user experience.

rules.lessonHeading: Git diff headers wrap file paths containing spaces
rules.message: Git diff headers wrap paths with spaces in quotes (e.g., +++ "b/path with spaces.ts"). Ensure your parsing logic accounts for the optional quote appearing before the a/ or b/ prefix.

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous execSync with piped stdio can cause the parent
rules.message: Avoid 'execSync'; use asynchronous 'spawn' with manual stream collection to prevent parent process hangs and ensure better lifecycle management.

rules.lessonHeading: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts
rules.message: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts like compiled-rules.json; fixes must be applied to the source lessons or the compiler logic to ensure they persist.

rules.lessonHeading: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor
rules.message: The @google/genai SDK constructor requires an options object { apiKey } rather than a raw string. Use: new GoogleGenerativeAI({ apiKey: '...' })

rules.lessonHeading: Centralize error signature detection (such as 429 status
rules.message: Use the centralized error detection utility instead of manually checking for 429 status codes or 'rate limit' strings (ensures consistency across SDK and shell-based paths).

rules.lessonHeading: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),
rules.message: Use path.relative(process.cwd(), path.resolve(process.cwd(), input)) for robust path normalization instead of manual string manipulation with process.cwd()

rules.lessonHeading: Always use fully qualified identifiers for caching
rules.message: Always use fully qualified identifiers (e.g., 'provider:model') for cache hashing and telemetry instead of just the model name to prevent cross-provider collisions.

rules.lessonHeading: Omit defensive XML escaping for prompt injection
rules.message: Avoid defensive XML escaping for trusted local data (like git diffs) in CLI tools to minimize prompt noise and clutter.

rules.lessonHeading: Setting open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml
rules.message: Set open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml to suppress routine updates while allowing security patches.

rules.lessonHeading: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')
rules.message: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only') when using environment variables for apiKey to bypass client-side validation for local-only endpoints.

rules.lessonHeading: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions
rules.message: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions or comments in XML tags explicitly labeled as 'untrusted content' within system prompts to prevent prompt injection.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for configurations where
rules.message: Avoid Zod's .url() validator; it requires a protocol prefix (e.g., http://) and fails on bare hostnames or host:port configurations.

rules.lessonHeading: For local LLM providers, 500 Internal Server Errors often
rules.message: Internal Server Errors (500) for local LLMs often indicate resource exhaustion. Include guidance to adjust parameters like 'numCtx' or check VRAM usage in the error message.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using exit 0 inside git hooks intended for chaining
rules.message: Avoid using 'exit 0' in git hooks as it terminates the shell process and prevents subsequent hooks from executing. Use conditional blocks (if/fi) instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Perform shell-level existence checks before invoking CLI
rules.message: Perform a shell-level existence check (e.g., '[ -f config.json ] && ...') before invoking Node-based CLI tools in git hooks to avoid unnecessary runtime overhead.

rules.lessonHeading: Detect existing hook managers and provide manual guidance
rules.message: Do not write directly to .git/hooks; this can clobber existing hook managers like Husky or Lefthook. Detect existing managers and provide manual integration guidance instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow
rules.message: Model name validation regexes should explicitly allow dots (.) to accommodate newer naming schemes like 'gpt-5.4'.

rules.lessonHeading: Use pnpm exec for workspace binaries in monorepos
rules.message: Use 'pnpm exec' instead of 'pnpm bin' to reliably resolve workspace binaries in this monorepo

rules.lessonHeading: Verify CLI availability in shared git hooks before execution
rules.message: Verify CLI availability (e.g., using 'command -v <tool>') before execution in git hooks to prevent brittle CI failures.

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility
rules.message: Resolve git root via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' instead of checking for a .git directory (monorepo/worktree compatibility).

rules.lessonHeading: Windows requires shell:true for git binary resolution
rules.message: Use 'shell: true' when calling 'git' via execFileSync to ensure the git binary is correctly resolved on Windows.

rules.lessonHeading: LLMs are notoriously poor at character counting; use
rules.message: LLMs are poor at counting; use semantic constraints (e.g., 'one to two short sentences') instead of numeric character/word limits.

rules.lessonHeading: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots for testing
rules.message: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots for testing (especially for prompts) to prevent brittle tests from minor prose changes.

rules.lessonHeading: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g.,
rules.message: Use matchAll() instead of match() to ensure all matches are validated; relying on the first match allows 'shadowing' where an attacker prefixes a payload with a safe match to hide a malicious one.

rules.lessonHeading: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating disjoint
rules.message: Include a space or delimiter between concatenated text fragments (e.g., `${a} ${b}`) to prevent 'keyword synthesis' and security filter bypass.

rules.lessonHeading: Use semantic constraints like "one to two short sentences"
rules.message: Use semantic constraints (e.g., 'one to two short sentences') instead of numeric character or word limits, as LLMs cannot accurately count them.

rules.lessonHeading: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots when testing
rules.message: Use granular assertions (e.g., toContain) instead of snapshots when testing prompts to ensure stability against non-functional prose changes.

rules.lessonHeading: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g.,
rules.message: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g., via matchAll) when performing security validations rather than checking only the first match to prevent shadowing attacks.

rules.lessonHeading: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating disjoint
rules.message: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating text fragments (e.g., use .join(' ') or `${a} ${b}`) to prevent keyword synthesis that can bypass security filters.

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the global ('g') flag
rules.message: Check for the presence of the global ('g') flag before appending it to an existing RegExp's flags; duplicate flags cause a runtime SyntaxError.

rules.lessonHeading: When implementing custom glob matching for dir/\*/.ext,
rules.message: When checking for double-wildcards ('**'), ensure the index check excludes 0 to prevent repo-relative paths from being misinterpreted as root-anchored.

rules.lessonHeading: In core packages, a small, tested custom implementation
rules.message: In core packages, a small custom implementation is preferred over adding 'micromatch' to keep dependencies lean.

rules.lessonHeading: Use occurrence counts of opening and closing markers
rules.message: Use occurrence counts of opening and closing markers instead of simple string inclusion checks to accurately detect corruption in lines with multiple or partial tags.

rules.lessonHeading: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files
rules.message: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files to ensure files located within submodules are included in the output.

rules.lessonHeading: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when
rules.message: The interactive 'pnpm changeset' command crashes in non-TTY environments like CI. For automated releases, write changeset files manually to .changeset/ or use non-interactive subcommands like 'version' or 'publish'.

rules.lessonHeading: Use --body-file for LLM text in CLI
rules.message: Use --body-file instead of --body for LLM-generated text to prevent shell injection and escaping issues.

rules.lessonHeading: ESM intra-module mock requires re-binding
rules.message: ESM intra-module mocks require manual re-binding. Spreading vi.importActual() retains original closure references for internal calls. Re-bind dependent functions manually (see packages/cli/src/utils.test.ts).

rules.lessonHeading: GCA may suggest reverting dynamic imports back to static
rules.message: Use dynamic imports for @mmnto/totem in CLI command files to preserve startup performance (avoid static top-level imports).

rules.lessonHeading: When manually parsing CLI arguments, verify that a flag's
rules.message: Simple indexOf lookups on process.argv are prone to errors. When manually parsing flags, verify the value exists and does not start with a hyphen to avoid interpreting the next flag as a parameter.

rules.lessonHeading: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates
rules.message: Shield gates and blocking hooks must use synchronous execution (e.g., execSync) to prevent race conditions. Asynchronous execution can allow the agent to proceed before the validation check completes.

rules.lessonHeading: Proactively remove references to planned features from user
rules.message: Proactively remove references to planned or upcoming features from documentation. Align user guides strictly with the current release state.

rules.lessonHeading: The Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist (GCA) do not
rules.message: Use 'GEMINI.md' (uppercase) at the project root. Gemini CLI and Code Assist do not recognize lowercase configuration files or the '.gemini/' directory.

rules.lessonHeading: Gemini is preferred over OpenAI for embeddings because it
rules.message: Gemini is preferred over OpenAI for embeddings because it supports task-type awareness, which yields superior retrieval quality in RAG applications.

rules.lessonHeading: Iterative string replacements for a list of identifiers
rules.message: Iterative string replacements in a loop are inefficient. Consolidate your list of identifiers into a single regex with an alternation group (e.g., /id1|id2|id3/g) to improve performance and maintainability.

rules.lessonHeading: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools
rules.message: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools can corrupt the stdio transport protocol. Use dedicated file-based loggers or return formatted system warnings.

rules.lessonHeading: Transitioning from generic errors to a typed hierarchy
rules.message: Use TotemGitError or a specific subclass instead of generic Error to support typed hierarchies and domain-specific recovery logic.

rules.lessonHeading: AI agent memory config architectures differ significantly
rules.message: .gemini/gemini.md (lowercase) is not read by Gemini CLI or GCA by default. Use GEMINI.md (root) for the CLI or .gemini/config.yaml for GCA.

rules.lessonHeading: Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based
rules.message: Do not hardcode max_tokens. Dynamically adjust it based on the model's specific limits (e.g., 4K for Haiku, 16K for Opus) to prevent API failures or unnecessary truncation.

rules.lessonHeading: When implementing custom environment variable loaders,
rules.message: When implementing custom environment variable loaders, explicitly strip surrounding quotes from values (e.g., .replace(/^['"]|['"]$/g, '')) to prevent configuration errors.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files
rules.message: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files. Use environment variables (via gitignored .env files) instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing errors in core modules hides silent failures,
rules.message: Avoid direct console logging in core modules to prevent environment coupling; use optional onWarn callbacks for diagnostic reporting instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Project conventions require a universal 'Totem Error' tag
rules.message: Project conventions require the 'Totem Error' tag for error logs and command-specific tags (e.g., 'Init:') for status logs to ensure filterability.

rules.lessonHeading: Placing imports inside command function bodies prevents
rules.message: Move imports inside the command function body (using dynamic import) to improve CLI startup performance by lazy-loading dependencies.

rules.lessonHeading: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies
rules.message: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies content or loses metadata. Use matchAll to capture heading indices for precise slicing.

rules.lessonHeading: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation,
rules.message: In SARIF generation, failing to find a rule index must trigger a hard error instead of falling back to a default (e.g., 0). Silent fallbacks cause violations to be attributed to the wrong rule definitions.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules
rules.message: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules. Move dynamic imports to specific command handlers to defer loading heavy dependencies and optimize CLI startup speed.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded default values for properties like rule
rules.message: Hardcoded rule categories or severities detected. Use shared constants from the core schema definition to prevent magic string inconsistencies between the reporting engine and CLI layers.

rules.lessonHeading: Automated gates like pre-commit or pre-push hooks
rules.message: Always use '--deterministic' with 'shield' in pre-commit/pre-push hooks to avoid high latency and operational costs of LLM calls.

rules.lessonHeading: When wrapping external errors or providing fallbacks,
rules.message: Error messages must start with the '[Totem Error]' prefix to ensure they are easily identifiable and searchable for end users.

rules.lessonHeading: PID-based process liveness checks (process.kill(pid, 0))
rules.message: PID-based process liveness checks (process.kill(pid, 0)) are unreliable across PID namespaces (Docker/CI). Combine PID checks with timestamp-based expiry logic for safety.

rules.lessonHeading: CLAUDE.md compliance experiment (2026-03-14) proved
rules.message: Keep agent config files lean (~40 lines). Verbose architecture and feature documentation suppress MCP tool usage. Move these to separate documentation files and use full MCP tool names (e.g., mcp__server__tool) in instructions.

rules.lessonHeading: Project conventions require log.error to use a mandatory
rules.message: Project conventions require 'log.error' to include a 'Totem Error' tag. 'log.warn' and 'log.info' must use command-specific tags and cannot use the 'Totem Error' tag.

rules.lessonHeading: Brace expansion (e.g., \*.{ts,js}) is not universally
rules.message: Brace expansion (e.g., {ts,js}) is not universally supported in globs. Expand these into an explicit array of separate strings.

rules.lessonHeading: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config
rules.message: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config files. Secrets must live in gitignored .env files and are inherited via the environment.

rules.lessonHeading: Lesson: Lesson headings are strictly limited to 60
rules.message: Lesson headings must not exceed 60 characters to serve as efficient SARIF identifiers and vector search labels.

rules.lessonHeading: Generating IDs from violation properties like file, line,
rules.message: Use deterministic IDs (e.g. hash of file, line, and content) instead of timestamps or random numbers to ensure stability across runs and reliable testing.

rules.lessonHeading: The LLM documentation generator consistently hallucinates
rules.message: Issue #515 (Claude Code hooks) was never implemented and is a known LLM hallucination. Remove references to #515; the feature is tracked under #520.

rules.lessonHeading: CLI command entrypoints should catch validation errors
rules.message: CLI command entrypoints should catch validation errors and print clean, user-facing messages instead of throwing raw JavaScript errors. Reserve throwing for internal library functions.

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports from heavy core packages
rules.message: Static top-level imports from heavy core packages can significantly slow down CLI startup. Use dynamic `await import()` inside the command's execution function instead.

rules.lessonHeading: When masking secrets with assignment patterns, use capture
rules.message: When masking secrets with assignment patterns, use capture groups (e.g., $1) or a replacer function to redact only the sensitive value. Replacing the entire match removes the key and assignment syntax, making the data harder to debug.

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting markers in user-authored files, use
rules.message: Use a line-start anchored regex (e.g., /^marker/m) instead of simple string search (.includes, .indexOf, or .match with a string) to detect markers. This prevents false positives if the marker text appears within the content body.

rules.lessonHeading: File system watchers should monitor for "rename" events
rules.message: File system watchers must monitor for 'rename' events to support atomic saves. Watching only 'change' (modification) events leads to stale caches when editors swap files.

rules.lessonHeading: Core library modules should accept an optional onWarn
rules.message: Core library modules should accept an optional onWarn callback instead of logging directly or silently swallowing errors to ensure callers can observe internal failures.

rules.lessonHeading: Explicit type assertions are often unnecessary when using
rules.message: Explicit type assertions are redundant when using the nullish coalescing operator (??) with a literal fallback. TypeScript's inference automatically resolves the narrowest union type.

rules.lessonHeading: Standardize exception messages with a consistent prefix
rules.message: Standardize exception messages by prefixing them with '[Totem Error]' to help distinguish between internal and external errors.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using partial matchers like toContain when testing
rules.message: Avoid using partial matchers like 'toContain' when testing secret masking or sanitization; use exact string assertions to ensure logic preserves context.

rules.lessonHeading: Using Array.prototype.find inside a loop results in O(N\*M)
rules.message: Using Array.prototype.find() inside a loop results in O(N*M) complexity. Index data into a Map before the loop for O(1) lookups.

rules.lessonHeading: When identifying directory-based glob patterns (like
rules.message: When identifying directory-based glob patterns, ensure the separator check excludes index 0 (e.g., use .indexOf('/', 1)) to prevent misinterpreting root-anchored paths as repo-relative.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using non-unique fields like headings to identify
rules.message: Avoid using non-unique fields like 'heading' to identify items for deletion. Use unique hashes or full content instead to prevent accidental data loss.

rules.lessonHeading: Moving instructions from the error message string
rules.message: Move instructions (e.g., 'Try running...', 'Fix: ...') from the error message string to a dedicated 'recoveryHint' property for consistent CLI formatting.

rules.lessonHeading: Standardized logging utilities should always be used over
rules.message: Use standardized logging utilities instead of console.error to ensure consistent output formatting and integration with the tagging system.

rules.lessonHeading: When deferring a bot optimization suggestion as "premature"
rules.message: When deferring an optimization as 'premature', you must document a concrete trigger condition (e.g., 'revisit when list exceeds N entries') to ensure it is eventually addressed.

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports prevent heavy dependencies from degrading
rules.message: Dynamic imports should be restricted to command entry points. Utility and adapter layers should remain straightforward and synchronous.

rules.lessonHeading: Convert top-level static imports to dynamic import() calls
rules.message: Convert top-level static imports to dynamic import() calls inside command handlers to reduce CLI startup latency.

rules.lessonHeading: Separate fast, pattern-based checks into a "lint" command
rules.message: Separate fast, pattern-based checks into 'lint' and deep AI-powered analysis into 'shield' to align command names with user intent.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation
rules.message: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation for initialization methods. Use a public static factory method to encapsulate asynchronous setup.

rules.lessonHeading: Use a case-insensitive regex with optional internal
rules.message: Avoid rigid closing tag matches which are easily bypassed. Use a case-insensitive regex with optional internal whitespace (e.g., /<\/\s*tag\s*>/i) to prevent prompt injection vulnerabilities.

rules.lessonHeading: Using a unified "Totem Error" tag in error logs instead
rules.message: Use the unified 'Totem Error' tag in error logs (e.g., '[Totem Error] message') instead of command-specific identifiers for consistent observability.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using console methods directly in core library
rules.message: Avoid using console methods in core library modules to maintain I/O separation; provide a callback (e.g., onWarn) so consumers can decide how to surface output.

rules.lessonHeading: The text-embedding-004 identifier is frequently unavailable
rules.message: The 'text-embedding-004' identifier is frequently unavailable for Gemini; use 'gemini-embedding-2-preview' instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Core library code should avoid direct calls to console
rules.message: Avoid direct console calls in core library code to prevent side effects in consuming applications. Use configurable callbacks instead.
